title: 3 patterns to build long-running AI agents
summary: Google Cloud Tech presents three architectural patterns for production-grade long-running AI agents: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. These patterns enable AI agents to manage multi-day workflows beyond stateless chatbot limitations.
writeup: Google Cloud Tech outlines three architectural patterns for building production-grade AI agents that run across multi-day workflows rather than stateless chatbot interactions: event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation. The patterns address how agents pause and resume on events, persist state through checkpoints, and handle evaluation as a distinct component, with subagents as a supporting concept. The focus is on the architecture required to sustain long-running tasks in production.
